entities.name: KUNJUKUNJU
entities.description: A rice cultivar from Kerala claimed to have been developed by a farmer innovator over 50 years ago.

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop species to which the Kunjukunju cultivar belongs.

entities.name: KERALA
entities.description: The state in India where the Kunjukunju rice cultivar originates.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the discussed legislations and grassroots innovations are located.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY ACT
entities.description: Legislation ensuring equitable sharing of benefits to conservers of biological resources and holders of traditional knowledge.

entities.name: NATIONAL INNOVATION FOUNDATION
entities.description: An organization that scouts and supports grassroots innovations developed without formal sector help.

entities.name: PROTECTION OF PLANT VARIETIES AND FARMERS’ RIGHTS ACT
entities.description: Sui-generis legislation offering legal protection to Plant Genetic Resources for Food and Agriculture and recognizing Farmer's Rights.

entities.name: PLANT GENETIC RESOURCES FOR FOOD AND AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Biological materials of plant origin used for food and agriculture that are protected by law.

entities.name: FARMER'S RIGHTS
entities.description: A component of the Protection of Plant Varieties and Farmers’ Rights Act facilitating the recognition of crop varieties developed by farmers.

entities.name: CASE ANALYSIS
entities.description: The methodology adopted to examine the opportunities and challenges of legislations using the Kunjukunju case.
